root_causes.cause: Host 172.16.0.12 is not responding (host down or host firewall blocking ICMP)
evidence: Alert: 25 ICMP pings sent to 172.16.0.12, 0 responses (alert_description)
R2 shows LAN address configured as 172.16.0.1/24 on GigabitEthernet0/0/0 — host IP 172.16.0.12 is in same subnet (facts: R2 GigabitEthernet0/0/0 address 172.16.0.1/24)

No router ACL explicitly denying ICMP to 172.16.0.0/24 in provided configs — R2_TO_R1 and R1_TO_R2 ACLs deny specific TCP ports then 'permit ip any any' (R2 access_lists: R2_TO_R1 seq20 permit ip any any; R1 access_lists: R1_TO_R2 seq20 permit ip any any)
R3 ACLs (BLOCK_FTP) apply to Loopback1 only and are FTP-specific; not relevant to ICMP to 172.16.0.12

root_causes.cause: R2 LAN interface or local switch/link to host is down/operationally failed (interface physically down, switchport down, or host disconnected)
evidence: R2 GigabitEthernet0/0/0 is present in running-config and not administratively shutdown, but only config shown (no operational state): "R2 GigabitEthernet0/0/0 address 172.16.0.1/24"
Alert shows no replies from host despite router LAN IP in same subnet — a physical/link problem on the segment would explain zero responses

root_causes.cause: Host 172.16.0.12 may be misconfigured (wrong IP/netmask/gateway) or otherwise not using 172.16.0.1 as gateway, so it doesn't reply to ICMP from the probing source
evidence: R2 LAN configured as 172.16.0.0/24 (R2 GigabitEthernet0/0/0 172.16.0.1/24); no host config available to confirm 172.16.0.12 settings
Absence of router ACLs blocking ICMP implies lack of replies is likely caused by host-side configuration or state

missing_info: Operational state and connectivity checks on the R2 LAN segment: output of 'show ip interface brief' on R2 (to confirm GigabitEthernet0/0/0 is up/up)
ARP and MAC visibility: 'show ip arp 172.16.0.12' and the access switch 'show mac address-table interface <port>' for the host-facing port (to confirm host is seen at L2/L3)
Ping/traceroute from R2 itself to 172.16.0.12 (to determine if the router can reach the host locally)
ICMP/filter counters: 'show access-lists R2_TO_R1' and 'show access-lists R1_TO_R2' (to verify no unexpected matches), and 'show ip access-lists' counters if available
Host-side status: confirm host is powered on and its IP/gateway/firewall settings (is ICMP allowed?)

data.intent_description: Host 172.16.0.12 is unreachable (25 ICMP pings sent, 0 replies). Restore local LAN link and L2/L3 reachability so the host can respond to probes.

plan.problem: Host 172.16.0.12 is not responding on the 172.16.0.0/24 LAN; likely host down, local link/interface down, or ARP resolution failure.
plan.fix_summary: Ensure R2's LAN interface is operational and force router to re-resolve the host's ARP entry so local connectivity can be re-established.

R3 ACLs (BLOCK_FTP) apply to Loopback1 only and are FTP-specific; not relevant to ICMP to 172.16.0.12

root_causes.cause: Host 172.16.0.12 may be misconfigured (wrong IP/netmask/gateway) or otherwise not using 172.16.0.1 as gateway, so it doesn't reply to ICMP from the probing source
evidence: R2 LAN configured as 172.16.0.0/24 (R2 GigabitEthernet0/0/0 172.16.0.1/24); no host config available to confirm 172.16.0.12 settings

Ping/traceroute from R2 itself to 172.16.0.12 (to determine if the router can reach the host locally)
ICMP/filter counters: 'show access-lists R2_TO_R1' and 'show access-lists R1_TO_R2' (to verify no unexpected matches), and 'show ip access-lists' counters if available
Host-side status: confirm host is powered on and its IP/gateway/firewall settings (is ICMP allowed?)

plan.problem: Host 172.16.0.12 is not responding on the 172.16.0.0/24 LAN; likely host down, local link/interface down, or ARP resolution failure.
plan.fix_summary: Ensure R2's LAN interface is operational and force router to re-resolve the host's ARP entry so local connectivity can be re-established.

messages.content:
What was observed
- 25 ICMP probes to 172.16.0.12 returned 0 replies (host unreachable from monitoring).

Diagnosis (main cause)
- Most likely host-level (host powered off, host firewall blocking ICMP, or transient host/link issue). Local LAN or host misconfiguration were secondary possibilities.

What I did / changes made
- Checked/ensured R2 GigabitEthernet0/0/0 is administratively up (it was already enabled).
- Ran reachability test from R2 to 172.16.0.12 (ICMP ping).

Verification
- Passed. R2 Gi0/0/0 is up/up with no errors, and ping from R2 to 172.16.0.12 succeeded 5/5 (100%).
- Remaining: none required at this time. If the issue recurs, collect R2 ARP/MAC-table and host-side status (power, IP/gateway, firewall).
